full_response:
Step 1:  Identify the relative speed  
The primed frame R′ moves with speed  
v = v′ = 3c/5 = βc                (along +x)  
Hence  
β = 3/5 , β² = 9/25  

Step 2:  Compute the Lorentz factor  
γ = 1/√(1−β²) = 1/√(1−9/25) = 1/√(16/25) = 5/4  

Step 3:  Write the standard Lorentz transformation (boost in +x–direction)  
For an event whose coordinates in R are (ct, x, y, z) and in R′ are (ct′, x′, y′, z′),  

ct′ = γ( ct − βx )  
x′  = γ( x − βct )  
y′  = y  
z′  = z  

Step 4:  Substitute the numerical values of γ and β  

ct′ = (5/4)( ct − (3/5)x ) = (5/4)ct − (3/4)x  

x′  = (5/4)( x − (3/5)ct ) = (5/4)x − (3/4)ct  

y′  = y  

z′  = z  

Expressing t′ explicitly (divide the first equation by c):  

t′ = (5/4)( t − (3x)/(5c) ) = (5/4)t − (3x)/(4c)  

\boxed{ \begin{aligned}
t' &= \frac54\!\left(t-\frac{3x}{5c}\right),\\[4pt]
x' &= \frac54\!\left(x-\frac{3ct}{5}\right),\\[4pt]
y' &= y,\quad z' = z
\end{aligned} }  

Confidence: 90%
